item.title: Asda might help break Ocado’s supermarket stalemate
item.description: Tie-up suggests the online grocery delivery group is nearing its goal after it struggled to win new partners

item.title: SpaceX and the ‘enshittification’ of markets
item.description: The IPO of Elon Musk’s company is a big risk for the millions of investors who have put savings into passive investing

item.title: Why Iran fears a deal today means more war tomorrow
item.description: Tehran interprets the US’s seemingly generous concessions as too good to be true

item.title: A bumpy road to electric vehicle success
item.description: Ferrari Luce’s launch caused more heat than light but has lessons for incumbents

item.title: Am I lower-value human capital?
item.description: Thanks to one CEO’s ‘inartful’ words, it’s a question we all need to consider

item.title: Charles Goodhart: ‘Demography is going to make life worse and worse and worse’
item.description: The leading economist warns that fiscal pressures will constrain independent monetary policy

item.title: Markus Braun’s six-year detention violates his human rights
item.description: The former Wirecard CEO has been detained since 2020 without a verdict — a length almost without precedent

item.title: Don’t bet on Trump reining in the prediction markets
item.description: The sector is expanding at breakneck speed in the US

item.title: Urgent clarity on policy needed on the energy transition
item.description: The impact of Middle East war is a reminder why certainty is crucial to spur a shift from fossil fuels

item.title: There’s a simple way for investors to dodge UK political storms
item.description: Predictably, British politicians think they can defy financial markets — one solution is to buy global

item.title: What if remote working, not AI, is to blame for weak junior hiring?
item.description: New evidence suggests the rise of working from home has made entry-level hires a less attractive proposition

item.title: Boston Museum of Fine Arts succeeds in reframing nature
item.description: A new show sees gardens as art and artists as gardeners — and sows big ideas to inspire us all

item.title: AkzoNobel should avoid painting itself into a corner
item.description: Dutch paint maker’s original tie-up with Axalta doesn’t look especially compelling

item.title: Caesars’ billionaire buyer hopes luck and leverage go hand in hand
item.description: The transaction comes at a time when Caesars and Las Vegas have been struggling

item.title: Britain’s expanding ‘lost generation’
item.description: Milburn report on youth joblessness should be a priority for whoever leads Labour

item.title: And just like that, the software apocalypse was over
item.description: Snowflake’s stock pop demonstrates what companies must do to escape the sector downturn

item.title: SpaceX IPO boldly steps into the unknown of AI economics
item.description: We still need to learn many things about the basic business model of frontier models

item.title: BP should never have made Manifold chair
item.description: The UK energy company’s board took an unwise gamble on a dominant executive

item.title: The Pope disrupts Silicon Valley
item.description: Unlike the US president, the pontiff is choosing to grapple with the serious challenges of AI

item.title: Who decides which jobs AI will take?
item.description: Different models are producing very different assessments of exposure levels

item.title: Trump is pushing Canada and Mexico towards China
item.description: The renegotiation of the USMCA deal is peripheral to the green tech revolution in world trade

item.title: Farage is facing his own menace on the right
item.description: The rise of Restore Britain may force Reform’s leader to switch strategies

item.title: Arsenal, PSG and the rise of the capital club
item.description: The Champions League finalists show how football’s old power centres have shifted

item.title: A tale of two EU-US comparisons
item.description: There is no well-defined single answer to whether the US has been outgrowing Europe

item.title: Semiconductors: supercycle or superbubble?
item.description: Plus, semis’ fragile supply chain
